seo.title: Mastra Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for Mastra agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams building AI agents with Mastra face immense compliance pressures due to handling sensitive PHI under HIPAA and HITECH, where even minor data leaks can trigger fines up to $1.5M per violation. Mastra's workflow-driven agents excel at patient triage and decision support but risk workflow validation failures, malformed tool outputs, and stale memory retrievals that could expose PHI or produce unauditable decisions. Vex provides essential guardrails as middleware to validate outputs, audit decisions, and ensure PHI de-identification before LLM processing.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects PHI from unauthorized disclosure and requires encryption, access controls, and audit trails for all patient data processing.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex intercepts Mastra workflow outputs to validate PHI de-identification, enforces encryption in transit, and creates immutable audit logs for every agent decision.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records and signatures be trustworthy, reliable, and auditable for AI-assisted clinical decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides tamper-proof audit trails for Mastra agent workflows and validates electronic signatures on AI-generated treatment recommendations.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notification requirements and higher penalties for PHI mishandling.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex automatically detects PHI exposure in Mastra tool calls and memory retrievals, triggering immediate breach alerts and containment.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated security controls over time for healthcare SaaS platforms handling patient data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex middleware continuously monitors Mastra agent pipelines, providing SOC 2-compliant evidence of security controls and data validation.

industryRisks.name: PHI Exposure in Workflow Transitions
industryRisks.description: Mastra workflow steps pass unvalidated patient data between transitions, exposing PHI to downstream LLMs.
industryRisks.example: Patient MRN #123456 leaks from triage workflow to billing agent, violating HIPAA.

industryRisks.name: Malformed EHR Tool Outputs
industryRisks.description: Mastra tool calls to EHR systems return inconsistent JSON, causing agent hallucinations about patient vitals.
industryRisks.example: Incorrect blood pressure reading leads to wrong medication dosage recommendation.

industryRisks.name: Stale Memory Context Injection
industryRisks.description: Mastra retrieves outdated patient history, confusing agents about current treatment status.
industryRisks.example: Agent recommends antibiotic despite recent allergy documentation in updated EHR.

industryRisks.name: Un-auditable AI Decisions
industryRisks.description: Mastra workflows lack comprehensive logging, making FDA Part 11 compliance impossible.
industryRisks.example: No audit trail exists for AI-generated discharge summary, blocking regulatory review.

implementation.title: PHI De-identification Middleware
implementation.description: Add Vex to Mastra workflow transitions to automatically de-identify PHI before LLM processing.

implementation.title: EHR Tool Output Validation
implementation.description: Validate all EHR API responses before Mastra agents process patient data.

implementation.title: Audit Trail for All Decisions
implementation.description: Capture complete audit trail for every Mastra agent decision with Vex logging.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Mastra in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Regional Health Network deploys Mastra agents for emergency department triage across 12 hospitals. Agents integrate with Epic EHR systems to assess patient vitals, medication history, and social determinants, generating preliminary diagnoses and treatment recommendations within 90 seconds. The workflow pulls live patient data via FHIR APIs, processes through GPT-4 for risk stratification, then routes high-acuity cases to on-call physicians.

Without compliance guardrails, the deployment immediately surfaced issues: raw PHI flowed unprotected between workflow steps, EHR tool calls occasionally returned malformed vitals data causing hallucinated diagnoses, and memory retrieval injected 3-day-old allergy data leading to dangerous drug recommendations. HIPAA auditors flagged the lack of de-identification and comprehensive audit trails during initial review.
caseStudyScenario.outcome:
Vex middleware integration resolved all issues within 48 hours. PHI de-identification blocked 100% of raw patient identifiers from reaching LLMs, EHR validation rejected 2.3% of malformed API responses, and stale memory contexts were flagged with 99.7% accuracy. The audit system generated FDA Part 11-compliant logs capturing every decision rationale, input data, and confidence scores.

Post-implementation, the system processed 18,000+ patients monthly with zero compliance violations. Triage accuracy improved 27% due to clean data flows, and physicians reported 40% time savings on chart review. HIPAA audit passed with zero findings, securing $15M in federal reimbursement contracts.

cta.heading: Comply with Healthcare Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex middleware to your Mastra agents today and eliminate PHI exposure risks with one line of code.
